data.narrative:
Crusoe Energy is a Series E AI & Software company founded by Chase Lochmiller and Cully Cavness, currently valued at $10B+ post-money, having raised $1.375B to date. The peer universe comprises 1 public comparable (PLTR) and 8 private peers in the same sector and stage, with particular reference to xAI, Groq, SandboxAQ, Ayar Labs, and WEKA.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier to the $10.0B last post-money valuation, implying $14.0B. Peer-median methodology across 6 same-stage, same-sector companies yields a median valuation of $4.7B, with a range spanning $1.1B to $250.0B—the wide spread reflects material dispersion in the AI infrastructure cohort, particularly the xAI outlier at $250.0B.

The triangulated range is $4.0B (low) to $16.1B (high), with a median of $9.3B. The current $10B+ valuation sits at the upper end of the peer-median distribution but below the stage-progression implied value. Key uncertainties center on the sustainability of xAI's valuation relative to comparable infrastructure plays and whether Crusoe's proprietary capabilities command a premium within the peer set. The wide peer range itself signals material dispersion in how investors are valuing this subsector at Series E.
